steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that joining an FTA increases a country's agricultural export growth rate. To weaken this claim, we need data showing the opposite: a country whose growth rate was higher before joining an FTA than after — the claim predicts higher post-FTA rates, so pre-FTA higher rates directly contradict it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — All the countries shown had positive growth in agricultural exports over the five years after joining their respective FTAs, but their rates of export growth varied.: Positive post-FTA growth would support the claim, not weaken it. ✗
  • Choice B — Although agricultural exports from Nicaragua grew over the five years after Nicaragua joined CAFTA-DR, their growth rate was even higher in the five years before CAFTA-DR.: Nicaragua's pre-FTA growth rate exceeded its post-FTA growth rate. That directly contradicts the claim that joining an FTA increases growth rate. ✓
  • Choice C — Although agricultural exports from Mexico decreased over the five years before NAFTA, a reversal in this trend was observed over the five years after Mexico joined NAFTA.: Exports going from negative to positive growth after joining NAFTA supports the student's claim. ✗
  • Choice D — Over the five years after El Salvador joined CAFTA-DR, agricultural exports from El Salvador grew at a rate of about 21.8 percent, which is higher than the rate over the five years before El Salvador joined the agreement.: Higher post-FTA rates support the claim. ✗
B is correct.
